lesson.subtitle: Lesson 6 -- Describing People & Places
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Ana Paula Porto de Oliveira Fornazari | Lesson 6 -- Describing People & Places

vocab.title: Match each word to its meaning
defs: a
friendly and confident, and enjoys meeting new people
b
happy to give money, time, or help to others
c
someone you can trust to do what they promise
d
relaxed and calm, and not easily upset
e
having a strong wish to succeed or achieve a lot
f
happy and positive in a way that people can see
g
not willing to change your mind, even when you are wrong
h
small, warm, and comfortable in a pleasant way
i
with a lot of open space inside
j
full of people, with little free space
k
full of energy, activity, and excitement
l
pleasant and attractive in a way that people like

paras: Everyone on my street knows Rosa. She is an outgoing and cheerful woman in her sixties, always ready to talk to a stranger. She is incredibly generous, and she never lets a neighbor go home hungry. People trust her completely, because she is one of the most reliable people I know. She is easygoing too, and I have never seen her angry. She can be a little stubborn about her old recipes, but that is part of her charm.

Rosa runs a small cafe on the corner, and it is the most charming place in the neighborhood. It is not spacious. It is a cozy little room with warm yellow walls and old wooden tables. In the morning it is quiet, but by lunchtime it becomes lively and crowded. Somehow, there is always space for one more friend.

Rosa was never ambitious about money. Her dream was simpler: a friendly place where people feel at home. When I describe her to my friends, I always say the same thing. She is a warm, generous old lady with a beautiful little cafe. And that short description says everything about her.
reading.source: Alumni by Better -- B1 reading, a description of a person and a place

reading.prompt: What is the main idea of the text?
choices: The text gives step-by-step instructions for opening a cafe on a corner.
Rosa is a warm, generous woman, and her cozy little cafe is the heart of the neighborhood.
The text explains why old recipes are better than modern ones.

items: Rosa is a shy, quiet person who avoids strangers.
The text says: 'She is an outgoing and cheerful woman... always ready to talk to a stranger.'
People trust Rosa because she is reliable.
The text says: 'People trust her completely, because she is one of the most reliable people I know.'
Rosa's cafe is large and spacious.
The text says: 'It is not spacious. It is a cozy little room.'
Rosa's main goal in life was to make a lot of money.
The text says: 'Rosa was never ambitious about money. Her dream was simpler.'

gapfill.text: In English, adjectives go BEFORE the noun: a cozy room, not a room cozy. When you use two or three adjectives together, they follow an order: opinion, then size, then age, then color, then origin or material, then the noun. So we say 'a lovely small old house', not 'an old small lovely house'.

bank.label: Useful sentences to describe people and places
items: She is a very generous and easygoing person.
He is outgoing and always cheerful.
You can really trust her, she is so reliable.
It is a cozy little cafe on the corner.
The square gets lively and crowded on weekends.
They live in a beautiful old house near the park.
My best friend is a kind, funny young woman.
The old town center is charming and full of life.

lf.text: Notice the order: opinion (lovely, charming, beautiful) comes first, then size (small, little, big), then age (old, young), then color (white) or origin (Brazilian, Italian). The noun always comes last.

items.situation: Describe your best friend in two or three sentences. What is he or she like?
Describe the place where you live. Is it cozy or spacious? Quiet or lively?
Think of a family member. How would you describe their personality?
Describe your favorite cafe or restaurant. What is it like?
tips: It gets lively and crowded when ...

items.situation: Describe a neighbor or a colleague you know well.

items: Describe one person you love, using three adjectives. Put the adjectives in the correct order.
Now describe a place you know well. Is it cozy or spacious? Quiet or lively? Use at least three adjectives.
Describe a person AND a place together, with no notes, using at least five adjectives from today.
A friend visits your city for the first time. Describe one person they should meet and one place they should see.

answerkey.title: Reveal the model description
list: Model: 'My grandmother is a warm, generous old lady. She is outgoing, easygoing, and always cheerful. She lives in a cozy little house near the sea. The living room is bright and spacious, but the kitchen is small. On Sundays the house gets lively and crowded, because the whole family visits. It is a charming, happy place, and I love it.'
Adjective order: opinion (warm, lovely, charming) - size (small, little, big) - age (old, young) - color / origin (white, Brazilian) - noun.
Adjectives go BEFORE the noun: a cozy room (not a room cozy).
With two adjectives of the same type, use a comma or 'and': a warm, generous woman / kind and funny.
answerkey.note: Adjectives come before the noun and follow a fixed order: opinion, size, age, color, origin or material, then the noun.

listenings.text: Let me tell you about my old friend Tom. He is one of the most easygoing people I know. He is outgoing, cheerful, and incredibly generous with his time. If you ever have a problem, he is the first person to help, and he is completely reliable. He can be a little stubborn sometimes, especially about football, but that only makes him more fun. After all these years, he is still the same warm, funny man I met at school.

Hi Ana Paula, it is Sarah. I finally found a new apartment, and I love it. It is a cozy little place on a quiet street. It is not spacious, but it feels warm and comfortable. The best part is the neighborhood. There is a charming old square nearby, and on weekends it gets really lively and crowded, full of music and food. I feel at home already. Come and visit soon. Bye!

extra_audio.text: My favorite person is my aunt Rosa. She is a generous and easygoing woman. She runs a cozy little cafe on the corner. In the evening, the cafe gets lively and crowded. Everyone loves her charming smile.
